assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to foreign currency exposure through an intragroup receivable or payable.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a hedge of the exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk, which is relevant once a corresponding receivable has been recognised.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge for variability in cash flows of a recognised item or a highly probable forecast transaction, and the context specifically addresses when foreign currency risk on intragroup transactions can qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
